name: Source Fidelity review for Transposon And Somatic Mutation Control bundle
summary: The revision 1 staged source, study, finding, activity, intervention, and outlook records are source-faithful. PubMed metadata and abstracts support PMID 41929344 as a medRxiv FTC/TAF post hoc DNA methylation biomarker preprint, PMID 42230995 as a Nature Aging DREAM/somatic-mutation mechanism paper, and PMID 42219647 as a recent TE aging review. Current ClinicalTrials.gov API records support NCT04500847, NCT07210528, and NCT06691217 as recruiting no-results registry records with the staged enrollment, phase, endpoint, and intervention boundaries. ARPA-H and Brown primary pages support the TPN-101/Censavudine program activity. No source-fidelity issue blocks editorial review.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 41929344 supports the staged FTC/TAF DNA methylation and epigenetic inflammatory-proxy values, but it is a medRxiv preprint and post hoc analysis of pharmacokinetic-study samples rather than a prespecified geroscience efficacy trial.
findings.why_it_matters: The source can support low-confidence human biomarker context, but not direct retrotransposon target engagement, functional benefit, disease modification, or durable aging claims.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the staged preprint, post hoc, young-adult, DNAm-endpoint, no-direct-TE-readout, and conflict caveats through editorial review.

findings.claim_or_issue: NCT04500847, NCT07210528, and NCT06691217 were checked against the current ClinicalTrials.gov API during review and still had no results section, but registry status is time-sensitive.
findings.why_it_matters: The staged no-results wording is source-faithful only while these records remain trial-watch activity rather than outcome evidence.
findings.recommended_action: Recheck all three registries before publication if the bundle is delayed materially, and preserve the no-results wording unless results are posted.

findings.claim_or_issue: ARPA-H supports the award amount, awardee, and aging-outcomes framing; Brown supports the Censavudine/TPN-101 healthy-aging trial plan and endpoint categories. Neither source supplies clinical results.
findings.why_it_matters: The activity item is valid program momentum but should not be interpreted as evidence of efficacy, target engagement, or safety in healthy older adults.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the staged activity wording and avoid converting the ARPA-H/Brown program description into a benefit-supporting finding.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 42230995 supports the DREAM mutation-burden, cross-species lifespan, Alzheimer pathology, and mouse knockout mutation-count claims, but the staged evidence is mechanistic and animal/observational rather than a drug-like human intervention.
findings.why_it_matters: DREAM belongs in the source-completeness repair as somatic-mutation mechanism context, not as clinical support for a deployable therapy.
findings.recommended_action: Preserve the staged wording that keeps DREAM separate from RTI and CHIP/canakinumab intervention evidence.
